elections.note: PLATEAU = last continuous election-night (overnight) report, NOT the 8 PM first tranche. Full Clarity/SOE ENR trajectory (event 43231, summary.html versions rendered from Wayback id_ raw replay): 109562 'Updated 11/6/2012 7:35:52 PM PST' = 279,357 ballots / 246 of 1000 precincts (pre-processed VBM first tranche, polls still open at the 7:35 PM data cut) -> 110598 11:13 PM = 302,373 -> 110675 12:09 AM = 326,340 -> 110760 2:01 AM = 392,784 (670/1000) -> 110768 2:38 AM = 404,135 (836/1000) -> 110787 'Updated 11/7/2012 4:14:04 AM PST' = 438,348 with 996 of 1000 precincts Completely Reported. 110787 is the LAST overnight report before a ~13-hour pause; the office resumed Nov 7 afternoon at 111043 'Updated 11/7/2012 5:14:09 PM PST' = 460,329 (1000/1000 precincts, the canvass-start; the +21,981 over 438,348 is the last 4 precincts plus the first canvass VBM batch, NOT precinct ballots), then the multi-day canvass jumped to 593,567 (11/10) and on to certification. By the Orange-County sibling rule (plateau = the overnight all-/near-all-precincts report just before the multi-hour gap to the next-day-afternoon canvass), 438,348 @ 996/1000 is the election-night plateau; the next-day 460,329 is the canvass-start (analogue of Orange's rejected next-day run). (v2 had wrongly used the 7:35 PM first tranche 279,357 = 42.76%.) Certified final 653,239 ballots (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: 195,354 poll + 457,885 VBM = 653,239; 79.87% of 817,837 reg). Pct = 438,348/653,239 = 67.10%. Slight floor (a possible un-archived 4:30-5 AM version could be a few k higher, but 996/1000 precincts means the count was essentially complete). Pre-epollbook (adopted 2020); ASV never. Snapshot used: Clarity ver 110787, internal Updated 11/7/2012 4:14:04 AM PST, Wayback capture 20121110090428.

CEILING (next-day all-precincts report; true overnight plateau modestly lower and not archived). The overnight election-night versions of Clarity event 54209 (Web01 layout; lower version folders 143630, 144518, 147xxx) WERE captured by Wayback as summary.html only -- their electionsettings.json/sum.json were never crawled, so they render blank and yield no readable count (confirmed by rendering 143630 = empty). The earliest version with archived JSON is 148095, sum.json top contest GOVERNOR 'BC' (county-wide ballots cast) = 251,620 with 1,066 of 1,066 precincts reporting, electionsettings 'Last updated 11/5/2014 5:08:58 PM PST' = the day-after-afternoon canvass-start (the analogue of 2012's 460,329 / Orange's rejected next-day run, all precincts at 100% plus the first canvass VBM batch). The true overnight plateau (Nov 4 ~midnight-Nov 5 ~2 AM, precincts just below 100%) is unarchived but ~3-5% lower by the 2012 overnight-vs-next-day ratio, est. ~239,000 (~59%). Reported value 251,620 is therefore a documented CEILING = 62.26%. (v2 left this null.) Certified final 404,166 ballots (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: 96,077 poll + 308,089 VBM = 404,166; 50.18% of 805,502 reg, the low-turnout 2014 midterm). Pre-epollbook; ASV never. Snapshot used: Clarity ver 148095, internal Last-updated 11/5/2014 5:08:58 PM PST, Wayback capture 20141106082527 (sum.json). v4 ADDITIONAL CHECK (no upgrade found): the morning-after PRIMARY route was exhausted -- Santa Clara County issues NO 'semi-official results' press release stating a ballot total (the county media-center/migrated press-release archive holds only pre-election advisories and post-certification 'Official Results Certified' releases), and no dated election-night / semi-official-canvass results-summary PDF exists on the county file host (only pre-election district-registration and VBM-returned-by-district reports and the certified SOV). The true overnight plateau is Clarity ver 147908 (the last event-54209 version before the 148095 next-day canvass-start); CDX of event 54209 confirms ONLY ver 148095 and the recent certified ver 149818 have archived sum.json/electionsettings.json -- the overnight versions 143630/144518/147095/147609/147848/147873/147908 captured only their summary.html shell + CSS/JS/images (no data JSON), so they render blank and the overnight count is unrecoverable. 251,620 therefore remains the documented CEILING (secondary). CORRECTION (2026-07-02 plateau adjudication): flagged comparable=false for stage consistency (next-day canvass-start ceiling, the same treatment as Riverside 2024); the retained 251,620 remains a documented CEILING on the plateau, not the plateau itself.

PLATEAU = documented FLOOR (UPGRADED from prior null via NewsBank San Jose Mercury News; Clarity event 64404 routes remain exhausted, see below). The Nov 9, 2016 Mercury News 'RESULTS' page (Bay Area News Group, p8S, NewsBank docref news/1608FF6D81BADB18, source 'Mercury News, The (San Jose, CA)') prints Santa Clara County's two countywide bond/tax measures at '41% of precincts': Measure A (housing bond) Yes 200,688 (67.0%) + No 98,820 (33.0%) = 299,508 total votes; Measure B (transit sales tax) Yes 215,336 (70.9%) + No 88,342 (29.1%) = 303,678 total votes (self-consistent cross-check: 200,688/0.670 = 299,534, 215,336/0.709 = 303,717, both matching the printed sums within rounding). Because virtually every voter completes a countywide ballot measure, this same-contest total is a reasonable proxy for ballots counted as of the printed report (RUNBOOK 'same-contest ratio floor'); the two measures bracket 299,508-303,678, so 303,678 (the larger, lower-undervote total) / 724,596 = 41.91% is adopted as a documented FLOOR (RUNBOOK 5.2): the report is timestamped to election night (compiled for the Nov 9 morning edition, i.e. Tuesday night's count), but well BEFORE the true overnight final. The companion 'COUNTING CONTINUES ON MAIL BALLOTS' article (Nov 10 edition, NewsBank docref news/160D491E5982E240, Sharon Noguchi) states elections officials estimated only 39 percent of Santa Clara County ballots remained untallied 'as of Wednesday afternoon' (roughly 61% counted by then, ~442,000 of the eventual 724,596), and that 'Santa Clara County's registrar finished tallying voting-booth ballots at 10:32 a.m. Wednesday' -- both describing counting that continued well past election night into Wednesday (POST-night, NOT adopted as the value: only a rounded verbal estimate, no exact ballot count or denominator given). The true election-night plateau therefore lies between the FLOOR 303,678 (41.91%) and this rough post-night ~61% estimate, consistent with the brief's calibration band (55-65%) for a clean 2016 plateau, but is not more precisely recoverable: the same-day-cycle 'ELECTION 2016 RESULTS' table (Nov 10 edition, NewsBank docref news/160D491E407C9688, p13A) shows Measure A/B at 100% of precincts (Yes+No = 419,916 and 425,863, about 58%), but that page reflects Wednesday night's canvass data (published Thursday Nov 10), a full day past election night, so it is NOT adopted either. Clarity ENR event 64404 (Web01 layout) remains unrecoverable for the night itself: the only election-night-period capture is a pre-poll-close placeholder (version 178468, Wayback 20161107003316 = Nov 6 4:33 PM PT, Ballots Cast 0); the earliest readable JSON is version 183112 (captured 2016-11-14), sum.json PRESIDENT BC = 602,338/724,596 = 83.1% (a deep canvass count 5 days post-election), not adopted. No county press release with a ballot total exists (media-center/migrated archive = pre-election advisories + certification releases only). Certified final 724,596 ballots (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: 190,379 poll + 534,217 VBM = 724,596; 82.79% of 875,176 reg, record turnout). Pre-epollbook (adopted 2020); ASV never. NewsBank access: SFPL ezproxy (Access World News), source 'Mercury News, The (San Jose, CA)', Newspaper/Text restricted search, Nov 9-10 2016 window, queries \"ballots counted\" Santa Clara / \"ballots cast\" county / registrar of voters ballots / precincts reporting Santa Clara County plus broader sweeps (ballots; Measure A Santa Clara County; Kalra Nguyen votes; election night results Santa Clara).

PLATEAU = documented CEILING, comparable:false (UPGRADED from prior null via NewsBank San Jose Mercury News; Clarity event 92418 routes remain exhausted, see below). Annie Sciacca and Joseph Geha, 'WHY BAY AREA COUNTIES STILL HAVE SO MANY VOTES TO COUNT' (Mercury News, Nov 8 2018 edition, p1B, NewsBank docref news/16F946169D59A048) state: 'In Santa Clara County, more than 75 percent of voters use mail-in ballots. As of Wednesday afternoon, about 304,000 ballots were counted and staff estimated roughly 270,000 still needed to be,' quoting Registrar Shannon Bushey; the county ran a '24-hour vote-by-mail processing system'. 'Wednesday afternoon' = Nov 7 2018, roughly 36-42 hours after Tuesday Nov 6 polls closed, i.e. POST election night (RUNBOOK 5.2 ceiling), NOT the plateau: the true election-night value is necessarily lower (ballot counts only grow with time). 304,000/625,425 = 48.61% is adopted as a documented CEILING, a substantial tightening of the row's prior known deep-canvass ceiling (443,266/625,425 = 70.9%, Clarity ver 221262, captured 2018-11-12, electionsettings 11/11/2018 4:43:32 PM PST, five days post-election): the new ceiling is from a report roughly one day post-election rather than five. Corroborated by Daniel Borenstein's op-ed 'TIME TO GET OVER OUR VOTING-BOOTH NOSTALGIA' (Mercury News, Nov 9 2018, p8A, NewsBank docref news/16FA96F533667668): 'As of Thursday morning, ... Santa Clara County had about 47 percent remaining' -- consistent with (likely not independent of) the Wednesday-afternoon ~47.03% remaining implied by 304,000 counted / 270,000 still-to-count, suggesting little additional counting was reported between the two press cycles and that both cite the same underlying county estimate. Neither figure is a precise 'N ballots counted' official statement against the certified denominator (both are 'about'/estimated, and their combined ~574,000-ballot estimated total undershoots the eventual certified 625,425), so confidence stays secondary; the true election-night plateau (Tuesday night into early Wednesday morning, before this Wednesday-afternoon snapshot) is not recovered, only bounded above by 304,000 (48.61%): well below the row's prior 70.9% ceiling, and below the brief's calibration expectation of a 50s-60s plateau, so the true plateau is most likely in the high-40s (just under this ceiling) to low-50s, short of a full clean 50s-60s plateau. No Nov 7 2018 Mercury News tabular 'RESULTS' page (analogous to the 2016 Measure A/B same-contest floor) was found in this pass: the Nov 7 front page pointed to a detailed results page (p A9) but it did not surface under alltext/title searches restricted to the Nov 7-8 window; 'STRONG SUPPORT FOR BOND TO FIX ROADS, BRIDGES' (Nov 7, NewsBank docref news/16F8F1C35E8A9A80) covers San Jose Measure T/V narratively ('early returns', 'too close to call') with no vote totals or precinct percentages, so no same-contest ratio floor was computable for this election. Clarity ENR event 92418 (Web02 SPA) remains unrecoverable for the night itself: NO election-night (Nov 6-7 2018) data version was crawled by Wayback; the earliest readable JSON is version 221262 (captured 2018-11-12), sum.json GOVERNOR BC = 443,266/625,425 = 70.9% (deep canvass, next version 222476 = Nov 20). The county's own official 'Post-Election Report for the November 6, 2018 General Election' states only the certified final and ballot-processing detail, no election-night count. Certified final 625,425 ballots (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: 138,468 poll + 486,957 VBM = 625,425; 70.61% of 885,764 reg). Still PRE-epollbook (county adopted e-pollbooks with the 2020 Voter's Choice Act vote-center rollout); ASV never. NewsBank access: SFPL ezproxy (Access World News), source 'Mercury News, The (San Jose, CA)', Newspaper/Text restricted search, Nov 7-8 2018 window, queries \"ballots counted\" Santa Clara / \"ballots cast\" county / registrar of voters ballots / precincts reporting Santa Clara County plus broader sweeps (ballots; Measure B Santa Clara County; results; \"of precincts\" Santa Clara).

PLATEAU = held election-night (overnight) level. Clarity event 115971 SPA: the Nov 8 election-night data JSON was not crawled, but the EARLIEST archived data version, 311769 (Wayback 20221109173423 = Nov 9 9:34 AM PT), is the held overnight plateau -- sum.json top contest GOVERNOR 'BC' (county-wide ballots cast) = 293,148 at 671 of 754 precincts (89%). With no county update between the ~1 AM Nov 9 last election-night report and this 9:34 AM crawl, 293,148 is the overnight level that held into the morning. The NEXT version, 312166 (Nov 9 7:37 PM), jumps to 309,580 at 754/754 precincts = the canvass-start (precincts pushed to 100% + more VBM), and the multi-day canvass then climbs to certification. By the Orange/2012 rule the held <100%-precinct overnight value (293,148) is the plateau, not the 100%-precinct next-evening canvass-start. (v2 had used the 8 PM first tranche / a Nov 9 running total and left this null.) Certified final 550,602 ballots (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: 40,694 poll + 509,908 VBM = 550,602; 54.55% of 1,009,422 reg; cross-confirmed by the county's 'Official Results Certified for the November 8, 2022 General Election' release). Pct = 293,148/550,602 = 53.24%. POST-epollbook (e-pollbooks + Voter's Choice Act vote centers since 2020); ASV never. Snapshot used: Clarity ver 311769, Wayback capture 20221109173423 (Nov 9 9:34 AM PT).

PLATEAU = held election-night (overnight) level. Clarity event 122582 SPA: the only archived data version is 353583 (Wayback 20241107034843 = Nov 6 7:48 PM PT), sum.json top contest PRESIDENT AND VICE PRESIDENT 'BC' (county-wide ballots cast; identical across all county-wide contests = total ballots) = 468,395 at 686 of 784 precincts (87.5%). Precincts still below 100% (the same signature as 2022's held overnight level, 671/754) means NO canvass-to-100% update had been posted by Nov 6 evening, so 468,395 is the Nov 5 overnight plateau that held flat through all of Nov 6 (San Diego-style day-after hold) before the multi-day canvass to certification. It is NOT the 8 PM first tranche (that would show ~0 precincts; 686/784 means election-day vote centers were counted = end of election night). Single archived snapshot, so the held level cannot be cross-checked against a second same-day capture, but the <100% precinct signal makes a hidden canvass batch unlikely. (v2 read 468,395 as a 'next-day running total' and left this null.) Certified final 765,495 ballots (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: 97,924 poll + 667,571 VBM = 765,495; 72.99% of 1,048,723 reg). Pct = 468,395/765,495 = 61.19%. POST-epollbook (e-pollbooks + VCA vote centers since 2020); ASV never. (2020 deliberately excluded as the COVID universal-VBM early-voting outlier.) Snapshot used: Clarity ver 353583, Wayback capture 20241107034843 (Nov 6 7:48 PM PT). CORRECTION (2026-07-02 plateau adjudication): the live Clarity CDN version history supersedes the held-flat inference above. Version walk: v353205 (11/5 11:57:23 PM PST, BC 459,487) -> v353227 (11/6 12:16:25 AM PST, BC 460,325 = the LAST election-night report, held through v353516 at 11/6 1:10 PM) -> v353583 (11/6 4:46:29 PM PST, BC 468,395 = a next-day canvass bump). The 87.5% precinct figure reflects Santa Clara's slow precinct rollup, not a frozen night state. Value corrected 468,395 -> 460,325; snapshot 20260702055736 of version 353227 used.

notes: v3 = PLATEAU metric (election-night % = the FINAL election-night ballots-counted report -- the cumulative total that plateaus late on election night / overnight and holds before the multi-day canvass resumes -- divided by CA SoS certified final ballots, Voter Participation Statistics by County 'Total Voters'). Santa Clara reports via the Clarity/SOE ENR system (results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Santa_Clara). The live Clarity server blocks scripted access (CloudFront 403) and only serves the current certified version, so all election-night data comes from Wayback (curl + headless Chrome bypassing the system proxy; archive.org is flaky and requires retries). DISCRIMINATOR used to identify the plateau: the held election-night/overnight level shows precincts just BELOW 100% (2012 996/1000 @4:14 AM; 2022 671/754 @Nov 9 morning; 2024 686/784 @Nov 6 evening), whereas the first canvass-start report pushes precincts to 100% AND adds the first late-VBM/provisional batch (2012 460,329 @1000/1000 Nov 7 5:14 PM; 2014 251,620 @1066/1066 Nov 5 5:08 PM). This mirrors the Orange County sibling rule (plateau = the overnight all-/near-all-precincts run before the multi-hour gap to the next-day-afternoon canvass). RESULTS: 2012 438,348/653,239 = 67.10% (primary); 2014 251,620/404,166 = 62.26% (SECONDARY -- only the next-day canvass-start was archived; a ~5% ceiling on the unarchived overnight plateau, est. ~59%); 2016 null/none (only Nov 13 deep-canvass JSON archived); 2018 null/none (only Nov 11 deep-canvass JSON archived); 2022 293,148/550,602 = 53.24% (primary); 2024 468,395/765,495 = 61.19% (primary). 2020 general deliberately excluded (COVID universal-VBM early-voting outlier). ADOPTION: e-pollbooks + Voter's Choice Act vote centers from the 2020 primary, so 2012-2018 = PRE-epollbook, 2022/2024 = POST; automated signature verification (ASV) never adopted, so vs_asv = 'n-a' for every election. This v3 file supersedes v2, which had used 8 PM first-tranche / running-total numerators (undercounting by ~2x) and left 2014/2016/2018/2022/2024 null. v4 PASS (no numeric changes): attempted to upgrade the 2014 (secondary) and 2016/2018 (none) rows to a PRIMARY morning-after plateau. Result: Santa Clara County does NOT publish a 'semi-official results' / 'semi-final results' press release stating a countywide election-night ballot total (its press-release/media-center archive holds only pre-election advisories and post-certification 'Official Results Certified' releases), and it posts no dated election-night / semi-official-canvass results-summary PDF (the county file host has only pre-election district-registration and VBM-returned reports, precinct consolidations, and the certified SOV; the 2018 official 'Post-Election Report' gives only the certified final, not an election-night count). The county's only election-night results channel is the Clarity ENR system, whose election-night data versions were never crawled by Wayback for 2014 (only the next-day canvass-start ver 148095=251,620), 2016 (only the Nov 13 deep-canvass ver 183112), or 2018 (only the Nov 11 deep-canvass ver 221262). All six rows therefore stand unchanged from v3: 2012 67.10% (primary), 2014 62.26% ceiling (secondary), 2016 null (none), 2018 null (none), 2022 53.24% (primary), 2024 61.19% (primary).
